concepts.title: Vaccine Development
concepts.description: Microorganisms have been extensively used in developing vaccines for various diseases. Vaccines like BCG for tuberculosis, OPV for polio, and HPV for cervical cancer have been developed using microbes.

concepts.title: Antibiotics
concepts.description: Antibiotics are substances that kill or inhibit the growth of microorganisms. Penicillin, streptomycin, and tetracycline are examples of antibiotics.

concepts.title: Microbial Nutrition
concepts.description: Microorganisms can be autotrophic (produce their own food) or heterotrophic (obtain food from external sources). Autotrophs like photosynthetic bacteria and cyanobacteria produce their own food using sunlight, while heterotrophs like bacteria and fungi obtain their food from external sources.

concepts.title: Types of Fermentation
concepts.description: To remember the types of fermentation, use the mnemonic 'EAT': Ethanol fermentation (for production of ethanol), Acetic acid fermentation (for production of acetic acid), and Lactic acid fermentation (for production of lactic acid).

concepts.title: Difference between Bacteria and Virus
concepts.description: Students often confuse bacteria and viruses. Bacteria are single-celled microorganisms with a cell wall and a defined nucleus, whereas viruses are obligate parasites that require a host cell to replicate. To avoid confusion, remember that bacteria can be seen under a microscope and have a cell wall, while viruses cannot be seen under a microscope and do not have a cell wall.

concepts.title: Standard Units of Measurement
concepts.description: To remember the standard units of measurement, use the mnemonic 'SI Units': Second (time), Ampere (electric current), Kelvin (temperature), and Meter (length).

concepts.title: Biofertilizers
concepts.description: Biofertilizers are substances that contain living microorganisms which, when added to the soil, increase its fertility. Azotobacter and Rhizobium are examples of microorganisms that are used as biofertilizers.

concepts.title: Antimicrobial Agents
concepts.description: Antimicrobial agents are substances that kill or inhibit the growth of microorganisms. These can be antibiotics, disinfectants, or antiseptics.
